content.claim: Roma students experience high school segregation in Hungary (62% attend predominantly Roma schools), are associated with lower upper secondary completion rates, and this segregation has increased since the 1980s alongside general school-choice mechanisms.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Education finance lens
limitations: Segregation research primarily documents residential and free-choice mechanisms, not 6/8-year gimnázium tracks as isolated causal driver
Linkage between early academic tracking and ethnic segregation is plausible but not quantified as direct causal pathway in retrieved sources
Roma completion gaps reflect multiple intersecting barriers beyond school composition
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
